nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia's work, including the album featuring "SLACK", is channeled through a record label with a pointedly memorable name.
nuggets.text: While operating with a strong independent ethos, Jamee Cornelia's 2023 album "Art School Dropout" was released via MindVomit Records. This partnership highlights a strategic choice for distribution and label support within the indie ecosystem, providing a framework for getting stylistically distinct projects into the world while retaining artistic control.

source.title: Same Difference (feat. Sonko & Sahtyre) - Jamee Cornelia: Song Lyrics, Music Videos & Concerts
source.quoteSnippet: Same Difference (feat. Sonko & Sahtyre) was released on January 6, 2023 by MindVomit Records as a part of the album Art School Dropout

nuggets.headline: While the specific producer for "SLACK" remains uncredited, Jamee Cornelia's discography reveals a distinct pattern of curated, track-by-track collaborations.
nuggets.text: Across their catalog, Jamee Cornelia consistently credits specific producers for individual tracks, such as Wino Willy on "Same Difference" and Raisi K on "SONIC." This suggests that the beat for "SLACK" was not an incidental choice, but likely the result of a deliberate producer selection to achieve its specific texture and mood, fitting a broader artistic method of pairing their flow with carefully chosen sonic architects.

source.title: SONIC prod Raisi K, by Jamee Cornelia

nuggets.headline: If the unique, slightly off-kilter groove of "SLACK" resonates with you, then the sprawling experimental hip-hop collective Temps is your next essential listen.
nuggets.text: Temps, a project spearheaded by the comedian James Acaster, shares a similar artistic DNA with Jamee Cornelia, blending hip-hop with a collage-like, genre-defying production style. Their album "PARTY GATOR PURGATORY" features a wide array of collaborators over textured, unconventional beats that will feel familiar to anyone who appreciates the confident, art-forward pocket Jamee Cornelia finds on "SLACK."

source.title: live from the bottom., by Jamee Cornelia

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a fiercely independent artist whose work occupies the space between experimental hip-hop and unfiltered self-expression. Leveraging direct-to-fan platforms and indie label structures, Cornelia maintains precise creative control, releasing a catalog marked by its confident delivery, textured production, and a clear, evolving artistic vision.
